title: Map words to their lengths
prompt: Write word_lengths(words) that takes a list of words and returns a dictionary mapping each word to its length. word_lengths(['hi','bye']) is {'hi':2,'bye':3}.

details.restate: Given a list of words, build a dictionary where each key is a word and each value is that word's length.

io.because: no words means an empty dictionary

terms.meaning: how many characters are in a word, from len()

help.plain: Make a dictionary where every word points to how long it is.
help.analogy: Like a coat-check tag on each coat — the word is the tag, its length is what's written on it.
help.mnemonic: word -> len(word), one entry per word.
help.nudge: How do you get the length of a single word (not the whole list)?
